and the white man at night. This
makes the colored man the boss The
operator working under the colored
man Is F. A. Browning. chief clerk to
E. c. Fleming. in the freight depart
ment. Operators say this is the first
time a colored man had been set
above a chief clerk on a railroad.


1t Is said all passing tracks be-
tween Wenatchee and Wilson Creek
on the Great Northern are crowded
with cars and in many cases trains
can not pass.


Word was received from President
Perham this morning which says he
has closed a number of important Of
fees which will offset any advantage
the railroad may gain.


Three relay men left the operators
and went back to the road this morn
ing.


Men who first went out and whose
families are in the east are leaving
for their homes. Several men were
secured jobs and their fare paid by
the o. r. I. today.
